summary: Frontend-only testing of Arabic RTL Industrial Control Lab Simulator completed via Playwright + HTML5 drag-drop dispatch into Konva workspace. All 13 specified flows passed: app loads with Arabic title and topbar, RTL direction set on root, sidebar visually on LEFT (x=0, workspace x=288), all 8 draggable components present, HTML5 DnD spawns components on Konva stage, max-limit badges turn red and disable draggable for buzzer (3/3) and contactor (3/3), Wi-Fi modal opens/saves/persists to localStorage and closes, Save session triggers JSON file download, Clear workspace shows confirm dialog and resets to empty state, Run with empty workspace shows error toast and does not start, Run after placing components switches to Stop and back. No console errors during normal usage.

issues: Mixed-content warnings: page is HTTPS but Wi-Fi relay calls http://192.168.1.100 are blocked by browsers in production. By design for local-network module, but worth documenting that browser console will show Mixed Content warnings whenever simulation is run/stopped. No functional impact on UI tests.

critical_code_review_comments: Wi-Fi relay calls go to http://... from an https page causing Mixed-Content warnings (line 226 App.js, fetch with mode:'no-cors'). Acceptable per spec (local network device) but consider documenting in UI that user must access app via http or via a proxy in production.

Timer Relay double-click test was skipped because Konva shapes are not standard DOM elements and cannot be reached via selectors - per the review_request instructions. The setTimerModalCompId handler is wired via Workspace -> CanvasComponent.onDoubleClick prop chain.
loadSession (line 172) auto-loads from localStorage first and only falls back to file picker if no saved data — could surprise users who want to load a file; consider separate buttons.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 13 reviewable flows pass. Konva-internal interactions (terminal click-to-wire, timer-relay double-click modal opening) cannot be tested via DOM selectors because Konva renders to canvas. Test approach used: dispatch DragEvent with custom DataTransfer onto [data-testid='workspace'] (HTML5 path used by Workspace.jsx onDrop). Wi-Fi mixed-content console warnings appear during run/stop but are expected.
